DynamicProvider implementa Provider y redirige atómicamente entre un
proveedor online y uno offline según el estado de la red.
Reemplaza el uso de *util.Ref[Provider].

name: SimpleAnimeMetadataWrapper
comments: A lightweight, flat wrapper that looks up episode metadata by plain episode number.
It is used by TMDB, Jikan, and AniList providers whose metadata map uses
plain string keys ("77") rather than composite keys.

name: NewProviderImplOptions
comments: NewProviderImplOptions configures the metadata provider.
If TMDBClient is set, returns a real TMDB-backed provider.
Otherwise returns the no-op stub.

name: TMDBProviderImpl

for per-episode metadata (titles, thumbnails, overviews, air dates).

name: BufferedWriter
comments: BufferedWriter queues DB write operations and flushes them in bulk.
This single-threaded writer prevents "database is locked" errors in SQLite WAL mode.

name: CleanupManager
comments: CleanupManager manages database cleanup operations to prevent concurrent access issues

name: SourceType
comments: SourceType is a typed string that identifies the origin of a playable media source.
Using a named type (rather than plain string) prevents silent failures from magic-string
mismatches when the frontend parses the JSON badge field.

name: EpisodeSource

fields.name: Type
comments: "local"

fields.name: URL
comments: direct-play HTTP path

fields.name: Path
comments: Absolute filesystem path (local sources only)

fields.name: Quality
comments: e.g. "1080p", "4K", "unknown"

fields.name: Priority
comments: PriorityLocal

fields.name: Title
comments: Human-readable label shown in the UI badge
EpisodeSource is the canonical, transport-agnostic descriptor for a single
playable source. It is the immutable JSON contract between the backend
decision engine and frontend player components.

name: EpisodeSourcesResponse
comments: EpisodeSourcesResponse is the top-level DTO returned by the episode sources endpoint.
PlaySource indicates which source type the player should auto-select.

LocalFile represents a single media file in the database.

name: MediaCollection
comments: MediaCollection groups movies or shows that belong to the same TMDB franchise/saga.
It is populated automatically when a scanned movie has a non-nil BelongsToCollection
field in its TMDB metadata.

name: MetadataCache
comments: MetadataCache stores raw JSON responses from metadata providers (TMDB, AniList, etc.)
to avoid redundant API calls across different scan sessions.

name: Event
comments: Event is the canonical envelope published through the Dispatcher.
Topic maps to an existing events constant (e.g. events.EventScanProgress).

name: InternalDispatcher
comments: InternalDispatcher is a thread-safe, in-process, topic-keyed pub/sub bus.
It connects internal modules (scanner, playback, …) to downstream consumers
such as the WebSocket hub without creating hard inter-package dependencies.

comments: may be nil when filler data is not yet loaded
IntelligenceService computes and caches episode-level content intelligence.

fields.name: Type
comments: "local_library"|"epic_moments"|"trending"|"essential_cinema"
CuratedSwimlane represents a single content row on the home screen.

name: CuratedHomeResponse
comments: CuratedHomeResponse is returned by GetCuratedSwimlanes.

name: FanArtEnricher

fields.name: lang
comments: preferred language code, e.g. "en", "es"
FanArtEnricher enriches a NormalizedMedia with HD logos, clearart, and thumbs from FanArt.tv.
It is NOT a full Provider (no SearchMedia) — it is an enricher called after a match is found.
FanArt.tv free API key: https://fanart.tv/get-an-api-key/
Rate limit: ~90 requests/minute

name: OMDbEnricher
comments: OMDbEnricher enriches a NormalizedMedia with ratings, runtime, director, and awards from OMDb.
It is NOT a full Provider (no SearchMedia) — it is an enricher called after a match is found.
OMDb free API key (1,000 req/day): https://www.omdbapi.com/apikey.aspx
Lookup requires an IMDb ID (tt-prefixed), which comes from the NFO or TMDb cross-reference.

name: OpenSubtitlesEnricher

fields.name: languages
comments: preferred language codes, e.g. ["es", "en"]
OpenSubtitlesEnricher searches for available remote subtitles using the OpenSubtitles v1 API.
It populates FileTechnicalInfo.RemoteSubtitles with available downloads.
Free API key: https://www.opensubtitles.com/en/consumers
Rate limits: unlimited searches, 5 downloads/day (without VIP)

name: ResilientProvider
comments: ResilientProvider wraps an existing provider with strict timeouts and error mapping.

name: TMDBProvider
comments: TMDBProvider implements the Provider interface using TMDB as the metadata source.

name: ParsedMedia

fields.name: Episodes
comments: Multi-episode support: [1, 2, 3] for ranges like "01-03"

fields.name: IsMulti
comments: Flag indicating multi-episode file
ParsedMedia contains the clean extracted metadata from a raw filename.

name: MatchingRule
comments: MatchingRule defines a rule for matching a filename or folder name to a Media ID
Example:
"pattern": "(?i)(.*)/(Mob Psycho)/(Season 1)"
"mediaID": 12345

name: HydrationRule
comments: HydrationRule defines a rule for attaching metadata to local files.
Example:
"hydration": [{
"mediaID": 12345,
"files": [
"pattern": "Mob Psycho - (\d+) - (.*)$",
"episode": "calc($1-12)",
"aniDbEpisode": "$1",
"type": "main",
"pattern": "Mob Psycho - NCOP(\d+)$",
"episode": "$1",
"aniDbEpisode": "$1",
"type": "nc",

name: FileDebouncer
comments: FileDebouncer is a thread-safe utility designed to deduplicate rapid,
successive file system events for the same file path.

name: EfficientDice
comments: EfficientDice is Sorensen-Dice implementation that minimizes allocs

name: FolderInfo

fields.name: SeriesName
comments: e.g. "Dragon Ball Z"

fields.name: Year
comments: e.g. 1989 (0 if not found)

fields.name: Season
comments: e.g. 2 (0 if not in a Season folder)

fields.name: IsMovie
comments: true if detected as a movie

fields.name: ExplicitProvider
comments: e.g. "mal", "tmdb", "imdb"
FolderInfo contains metadata extracted from the folder structure (Kodi/Standard style).

name: MovieMapping
comments: MovieMapping defines keyword-based rules to match a movie title to a TMDB ID.
Keywords:  ALL must be present (AND logic).
AnyOf:     at least ONE must be present (OR logic); empty = not checked.
Excluded:  if ANY is present the mapping is skipped.

name: SeriesMapping
comments: SeriesMapping defines keyword rules to match a series title to a TMDB ID.

name: FranchiseDef
comments: FranchiseDef describes a media franchise (Dragon Ball, Naruto, One Piece, etc.)
with its ordered list of movie and series mappings.
Resolution order:
1. Check that ALL TriggerWords are present in the title (fast-exit if not).
2. Iterate Movies in order; first match wins → returns (tmdbID, isMovie=true).
3. Iterate Series in order; first match wins → returns (tmdbID, isMovie=false).

name: FileHydrator

fields.name: LocalFiles
comments: Local files to hydrate

fields.name: AllMedia
comments: All media used to hydrate local files

fields.name: ScanLogger
comments: optional

fields.name: ScanSummaryLogger
comments: optional

fields.name: ForceMediaId
comments: optional - force all local files to have this media ID
FileHydrator hydrates the metadata of all (matched) LocalFiles.
LocalFiles should already have their media ID hydrated.

name: Matcher
comments: AgentMatcher acts as the autonomous "Brain" of the scanning engine.
Instead of binary matching, it uses Bayesian Probabilities to evaluate file paths,
contextual folders, and metadata to establish confidence in an identity match.

fields.name: TmdbClient
comments: Optional: TMDb client for enriching media synonyms

fields.name: NormalizedTitlesCache
comments: mediaID -> normalized titles

comments: legacy

fields.name: romTitles

fields.name: synonyms

comments: Media IDs that are not in the user's collection
MediaFetcher holds all media that will be used for the comparison process

fields.name: Type
comments: e.g. "tmdb", "imdb", "tvdb"

fields.name: Value
comments: The actual provider ID
NfoUniqueID represents a Kodi/Jellyfin <uniqueid type="tmdb">12345</uniqueid> element.
Multiple providers can appear in the same NFO file.

comments: Jellyfin/Kodi-style: <uniqueid type="tmdb">603</uniqueid>

fields.name: ID
comments: Fallback / TMDB ID usually

comments: TMDB ID

NfoFile represents the root of a Kodi/Standard NFO file.
Supports tvshow, movie, anime, and episodedetails roots.

name: WorkerPool
comments: WorkerPool is a bounded goroutine pool for CPU-heavy scan tasks.
All tasks share the same context; once ctx is cancelled in-flight tasks are
skipped and no new tasks are accepted.
Usage:
pool := NewWorkerPool(ctx, runtime.NumCPU()*2)
for _, path := range paths {
pool.Submit(func(ctx context.Context) { process(ctx, path) })
pool.Wait()

It uses a combination of fast internal parsers (mkvparser) and FFprobe as a fallback.

name: EBMLElement

fields.name: ID
comments: The element ID that identifies the type of element

fields.name: Size
comments: The size of the element's data in bytes

fields.name: Data
comments: The raw data contained within the element
EBMLElement represents an EBML element with its ID, size, and data.
An EBML element is the basic building block of EBML files. Each element consists of:
- ID: A variable-length integer that identifies the type of element
- Size: A variable-length integer that specifies the size of the element's data
- Data: The actual data contained within the element
The EBMLElement struct provides methods to extract different types of data from the element,
such as integers, floats, strings, and raw bytes.

name: EBMLReader

fields.name: r
comments: The underlying reader for the EBML data

fields.name: pos
comments: The current position in the stream
EBMLReader provides methods for reading EBML data from a stream.
EBMLReader is the main type used for parsing EBML data. It wraps an io.ReadSeeker
and provides methods to read EBML elements, variable-length integers, and other
EBML-specific data structures.
Example usage:
file, err := os.Open("video.mkv")
if err != nil {
log.Fatal(err)
defer file.Close()
reader := NewEBMLReader(file)
element, err := reader.ReadElement()
if err != nil {
log.Fatal(err)
fmt.Printf("Element ID: 0x%X, Size: %d\n", element.ID, element.Size)

name: EBMLHeader

fields.name: Version
comments: The version of EBML parser used to create the file

fields.name: ReadVersion
comments: The minimum EBML version needed to parse this file

fields.name: MaxIDLength
comments: The maximum length of an EBML ID in bytes

fields.name: MaxSizeLength
comments: The maximum length of an EBML size in bytes

fields.name: DocType
comments: A string that describes the type of document (e.g., "matroska")

fields.name: DocTypeVersion
comments: The version of the document type

fields.name: DocTypeReadVersion
comments: The minimum version of the document type parser needed to read this file
EBMLHeader represents the EBML header containing metadata about the file.
The EBML header is the first element in an EBML file and contains information
about how to parse the rest of the file. It includes the EBML version, document type,
and other metadata that helps parsers understand the structure of the file.
Example usage:
reader := NewEBMLReader(file)
header, err := reader.ReadEBMLHeader()
if err != nil {
log.Fatal(err)
fmt.Printf("DocType: %s, Version: %d\n", header.DocType, header.DocTypeVersion)

name: Demuxer
comments: Demuxer is a Matroska demuxer using pure Go implementation.
The Demuxer struct provides the main interface for parsing and reading Matroska files.
It encapsulates the underlying parser and provides methods for accessing track information,
file metadata, and reading media packets. The Demuxer can work with both seekable and
non-seekable input streams.
For seekable inputs, use NewDemuxer. For non-seekable streams (like network streams),
use NewStreamingDemuxer.

name: MatroskaParser
comments: MatroskaParser represents a parser for Matroska and WebM files.
It provides functionality to parse Matroska container files, extract metadata,
and read media packets. The parser maintains state information about the file
structure, including the EBML header, segment information, tracks, and other
metadata elements.
The parser can operate in two modes:
- With seeking enabled (noSeeking=false): Allows for more efficient parsing
by seeking to specific positions in the file when needed.
- With seeking disabled (noSeeking=true): Parses the file sequentially,
which is useful for streaming or non-seekable input sources.
After creating a parser with NewMatroskaParser, you can access file information,
track information, and read media packets using the provided methods.

name: SegmentElement
comments: SegmentElement represents the main segment element in a Matroska file.
The segment is the top-level element in a Matroska file that contains all
the actual data, including metadata, tracks, clusters, and other elements.
It is the largest element in the file and contains all other elements except
for the EBML header.
The Position field indicates the byte offset from the beginning of the file
where the segment element starts, and the Size field indicates the total size
of the segment element in bytes.

name: Packet
comments: Packet contains a demuxed packet from a Matroska file.
A Packet represents a single unit of media data that has been extracted (demuxed) from
a Matroska container. It contains all the necessary information to process the media data,
including timing information, track association, and the actual data payload.

name: TrackInfo

comments: TrackInfo contains information about a track in a Matroska file.
A TrackInfo structure holds all metadata and configuration information for a single
track within a Matroska file. This includes general track properties, codec information,
and type-specific settings for video, audio, or subtitle tracks.

CbSubsamplingVert uint32

ChromaSitingVert uint32

fields.usedTypescriptType: { MatrixCoefficients: number; BitsPerChannel: number; ChromaSubsamplingHorz: number; ChromaSubsamplingVert: number; CbSubsamplingHorz: number; CbSubsamplingVert: number; ChromaSitingHorz: number; ChromaSitingVert: number; Range: number; TransferCharacteristics: number; Primaries: number; MaxCLL: number; MaxFALL: number; MasteringMetadata: { PrimaryRChromaticityX: number; PrimaryRChromaticityY: number; PrimaryGChromaticityX: number; PrimaryGChromaticityY: number; PrimaryBChromaticityX: number; PrimaryBChromaticityY: number; WhitePointChromaticityX: number; WhitePointChromaticityY: number; LuminanceMax: number; LuminanceMin: number; }; }

comments: TrackInfo contains information about a track in a Matroska file.
A TrackInfo structure holds all metadata and configuration information for a single
track within a Matroska file. This includes general track properties, codec information,
and type-specific settings for video, audio, or subtitle tracks.

name: TrackInfo_Audio
comments: TrackInfo contains information about a track in a Matroska file.
A TrackInfo structure holds all metadata and configuration information for a single
track within a Matroska file. This includes general track properties, codec information,
and type-specific settings for video, audio, or subtitle tracks.

name: SegmentInfo
comments: SegmentInfo contains file-level (segment) information about a Matroska stream.
A SegmentInfo structure holds metadata about the entire Matroska file or segment.
This includes general information like title, duration, creation date, and
relationships to other files in a sequence.

name: Attachment
comments: Attachment contains information about a Matroska attachment.
Matroska files can contain attached files, such as fonts, images, or other metadata.
The Attachment structure holds information about these attached files, including
their location, size, and metadata.

name: ChapterDisplay
comments: ChapterDisplay contains display information for a given Chapter.
A ChapterDisplay structure holds the human-readable information for a chapter,
including its name, language, and country association. This allows chapters
to be displayed in multiple languages and with country-specific variations.

name: ChapterCommand
comments: ChapterCommand represents a command associated with a chapter.
Chapter commands are used to execute specific actions at certain times during
chapter playback. This can be used for interactive content or special effects.

name: ChapterProcess
comments: ChapterProcess contains processing information for a chapter.
Chapter processes are used to apply special processing to chapters,
such as codec-specific processing or command execution.

name: Chapter
comments: Chapter contains all information about a Matroska chapter.
A Chapter structure represents a chapter or section within a Matroska file.
Chapters can be nested to create a hierarchical structure, and can contain
display information, processing commands, and timing information.

name: Cue
comments: Cue contains all information about a Matroska cue.
Cues are indexing points in a Matroska file that allow for efficient seeking.
A Cue structure contains the timing and position information needed to locate
specific points in the media stream.

name: Target
comments: Target contains information about a tag's target.
A Target structure identifies what a Matroska tag applies to.
Tags can be applied to tracks, chapters, attachments, or editions.

name: SimpleTag
comments: SimpleTag contains a simple Matroska tag.
A SimpleTag structure represents a single key-value metadata tag.
These tags can be used to store information like title, artist, album, etc.

name: Tag
comments: Tag contains all information relating to a Matroska tag.
A Tag structure represents a collection of metadata tags that can be applied
to various targets within a Matroska file. Tags are used to store metadata
like titles, descriptions, and other information about the content.

comments: TestEnv is a centralized test environment that shares database,
logger, and file cache across test packages.

comments: ThumbnailCache provides a concurrent-safe, memory-bounded LRU cache

name: CachedReadSeeker

fields.name: src
comments: underlying source

fields.name: cache
comments: bytes read so far

fields.name: pos
comments: current read position
CachedReadSeeker wraps an io.ReadSeekCloser and caches bytes as they are read.
It implements io.ReadSeeker, allowing seeking within the already-cached
range without hitting the underlying reader again.
Additional reads beyond the cache will append to the cache automatically.

name: FileStream
comments: FileStream saves a HTTP file being streamed to disk.
It allows multiple readers to read the file concurrently.
It works by being fed the stream from the HTTP response body. It will simultaneously write to disk and to the HTTP writer.

name: Range

HttpReadSeeker implements io.ReadSeeker for HTTP responses
It allows seeking within an HTTP response by using HTTP Range requests

name: EdgeHTTPClient
comments: EdgeHTTPClient is a unified, generic HTTP wrapper designed to eliminate
redundant parser logic and external dependencies (like resty).
It enforces strict timeouts, context propagation, and zero-cost abstraction payload decoding.

name: ResponseMeta
